name: Resource Conversion
description: A controlled exchange that turns one resource, currency, or material set into another at a defined rate.
design_notes: Resource conversion is a pressure valve for uneven drops and long-term economy balance. It needs explicit input_resource_id, output_resource_id, conversion ratios, fees, limits, and rollback behavior so it helps players without creating infinite value loops.
design_purpose: Let players recover value from surplus resources or unlucky drops.
Connect separate economy loops without making every resource interchangeable.
Provide designers with a tunable sink, faucet, or catch-up route.
player_fantasy: The player feels clever for turning excess stock into the resource they actually need.

parameters.name: input_resource_id
parameters.description: Resource consumed by the conversion transaction.

parameters.name: conversion_ratio_numerator
parameters.description: Output quantity component of the conversion rate.

parameters.name: conversion_ratio_denominator
parameters.description: Input quantity component of the conversion rate.

parameters.name: daily_conversion_limit
parameters.description: Optional cap used to prevent one route from dominating the economy.

edge_cases: A conversion produces fractional output and must define rounding, remainder banking, or rejection rules.
Input and output resources are both tradable, allowing circular conversion through another vendor.
Conversion limits reset by day, event, account, or save slot and need clear persistence boundaries.
A content patch retires an event token while players still hold input_resource_id balances.
common_bugs: The UI preview uses rounded values while the transaction consumes exact values and grants a different amount.
Conversion consumes input before checking output inventory capacity, losing resources on failure.
Daily limits are stored client-side and reset by clock changes or reinstalling the game.
Recipes compare localized resource names rather than stable input_resource_id and output_resource_id.
balancing_notes: Conversion ratios should usually lose some value unless the goal is explicit catch-up or accessibility smoothing.
Cap or tax routes that convert common drops into rare progression resources, or rare sinks will collapse.
Monitor conversion volume by recipe_id to find resources that are too abundant or too hard to use.
accessibility_notes: Show input, output, fee, and remaining limit in the same confirmation view.
Offer max-convert and stepper controls that are keyboard/gamepad friendly and do not require repeated clicks.

implementation_notes.unity: Model conversion recipes as data assets with input_resource_id and output_resource_id; commit through one inventory transaction that validates counts, capacity, limits, and rollback.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep conversion math in pure functions and use an Autoload inventory service to apply resource diffs after limit and capacity checks pass.
implementation_notes.unreal: Run conversion on the server-side economy component for multiplayer; replicate committed resource diffs and remaining daily_conversion_limit to UI.
implementation_notes.web: Represent conversion as an immutable transaction diff and store recipe_id usage counters with the save or account state to prevent limit resets.

example_games.title: Warframe
example_games.note: Uses multiple resources and crafting routes that convert gathered materials into equipment.

example_games.title: Stardew Valley
example_games.note: Uses machines that transform raw goods into higher-value outputs.
